title: Earnings call transcript: Hut 8 Q1 2026 reports major revenue growth
text: Hut 8 Q1 2026 Earnings Call Summary: Revenue reached $71 million, representing a 226% year-over-year increase. Gross margins expanded significantly to 64%, up from 14% in the prior year. Net loss of $253.1 million, primarily driven by unrealized mark-to-market adjustments on digital assets. Adjusted EBITDA loss of $250.5 million. Segment Performance: Compute segment revenue tripled to approximately $66 million from $16.1 million. Compute segment margins expanded to 67% from 16%. Digital Infrastructure segment revenue remained flat at $1.3 million. Power segment revenue declined to $3.7 million from $4.4 million following the sale of the Far North portfolio. EPS came in at -$0.362, beating the forecast of -$0.4088 by 11.45%, though revenue missed expectations. Major Strategic Announcements: Beacon Point AI Data Center Campus Phase 1 commercialized with 352 megawatts of IT capacity. 15-year triple-net lease worth $9.8 billion base term contract value. Riverbend Project Financing: $3.25 billion investment-grade senior secured bond offering. BBB- rating from S&P and Fitch. Management Guidance: Q2 2026 EPS forecast -$0.54. Q3 2026 EPS forecast -$0.63. Development pipeline of 8.4 gigawatts in development. Parent-level liquidity of approximately $1.3 billion.
